evals.prompt: Write alt text for a static campus map image.
evals.expected_output: Describes the map's purpose and key locations in concise alt text rather than visual appearance.
expectations: The map has a text alternative
The alt text describes purpose and key locations
The alt text is not just visual appearance
The information is available without the image

evals.prompt: Build an interactive map embed that users can skip and identify by title.
evals.expected_output: Provides a skip link before the map and a descriptive title on the iframe or map container.
expectations: The interactive map can be skipped
The map is labelled descriptively
The map is identifiable to screen reader users
The skip target contains structured location details

evals.prompt: Write keyboard controls for map zoom and pan.
evals.expected_output: Includes arrow keys for pan, plus and minus buttons for zoom, and Escape for closing popups.
expectations: The map is keyboard operable
Zoom controls are exposed as buttons
Pan controls are available via arrow keys
Popups can be closed with Escape

evals.prompt: Create a structured text alternative for a map with listed locations.
evals.expected_output: Uses an adjacent accessible table or list so the essential location information is available independently of the map.
expectations: The map has a structured text alternative
The locations are readable without the visual map
The alternative is adjacent and accessible
The information is not map-only

evals.prompt: Write CSS for route legends and map markers so colour is not the only cue.
evals.expected_output: Adds text labels, patterns, or icons alongside colour-coded routes and markers.
expectations: Colour is paired with another cue
The legend has text labels
The map remains understandable in forced colours
The marker or route meaning is not colour-only
